description:
Opt-in diagnostic. Independent of the rest; can land any time.

Under the reworked epic (.5 is load-bearing, .3 dissolved), the
diagnostic reads from `module.callsite_outcomes` instead of
replicating the resolve. Warn when the typer's published Emitted
target selects the any-key spec (`[any; arity]`) for a callee
that also has narrower specs registered.

## Trigger condition

For each `CallsiteOutcome::Emitted { target: (fid, key) }` in
`module.callsite_outcomes`:
- If `key` equals `[Descr::any(); arity]` AND
- there exists at least one other registered spec for `fid` with
  a narrower key,
- emit `warning[type/spec-quality]: this callsite dispatches to
  the generic spec while N narrower specs exist`.

## Diagnostic surface

Walk `module.callsite_outcomes` after the typer + reducer
converge. Sibling to `collect_diagnostics` at ir_typer.rs:2520.
Gated on `FZ_WSPEC_QUALITY=1` env var so it doesn't disturb
standard build output. CI can opt in on curated fixtures.

## Diagnostic content

For each warned callsite, include:
- The callsite location (caller fn, block, slot — derive span if
  available).
- The inferred-arg Descrs at this site (from the outcome's key).
- The list of narrower spec keys registered for the callee.
- A help hint: "either widen one of the narrow specs to cover
  this site, or add an @spec to the caller to force narrowing."

## Dependency

Depends on fz-rcp.5 conceptually (the diagnostic reads outcomes;
in the pre-.5 world it would read resolve results). The data the
diagnostic needs (Emitted outcomes) already exists today via
fz-9pr — so this could theoretically land BEFORE .5 against the
current outcome map. Cleanest to land after .5 so the diagnostic
matches the new architecture.

## Acceptance

- Enabling `FZ_WSPEC_QUALITY=1` on fib_tailrec produces the
  expected diagnostic pointing at any-key dispatch sites.
- No effect when disabled (default off).
- Unit test for diagnostic content shape.
- 710+ unit + 168 fixture-matrix tests green (flag disabled).
title: fz-rcp.6: --Wspec-quality diagnostic — opt-in specialization-quality warning
